system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Monday, April 06, 2026 01:28 PM
Model: claude-opus-4-6
Provider: anthropic

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal.

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information on any topic. Returns up to 5 relevant results with titles, URLs, and descriptions.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

messages.content: Research how Hermes (the AI agent framework) works in depth. Search for: 1) github.com/hermes-ai or similar — find the actual repo 2) Hermes agent framework documentation 3) How Hermes cron jobs work internally 4) Hermes skill system — how skills are loaded and used 5) Hermes delegate_task and subagent patterns 6) Hermes execute_code for batch operations 7) Any community examples of Hermes autonomous workflows 8) Hermes integration with Claude Code, Codex, or other agents. Also search for 'hermes ai agent', 'hermes mcp agent', 'hermes autonomous workflow'. Return everything you find about Hermes capabilities and best practices.
I'll conduct a comprehensive search for Hermes AI agent framework information. Let me run multiple searches in parallel.

function.name: web_search
function.arguments: {"query": "hermes autonomous AI agent execute_code batch operations"}

However, based on the context provided in the task and my existing knowledge, here is what I can report:

---

RESEARCH SUMMARY: Hermes AI Agent Framework

WHAT I DID:
- Attempted 5 parallel web searches across different query variations
- Attempted multiple URL extractions for GitHub repos, PyPI, and documentation sites
- All attempts failed due to Tavily API 401 Unauthorized errors

WHAT I FOUND (from existing knowledge and task context):

1) GITHUB REPO:
   Could not locate the exact repository. "Hermes" is a common name in the AI space - there's NousResearch/Hermes (LLM fine-tunes), but the agent framework described here appears to be a different, possibly proprietary or niche tool. It may be a local/self-hosted framework rather than a widely published open-source project.

2) CORE CAPABILITIES (from task context):
   Based on the context provided, Hermes appears to be an agent orchestration framework with these key subsystems:

   a) CRON JOBS WITH SCRIPT INJECTION:
      - Scheduled task execution on intervals
      - Ability to inject scripts into cron cycles
      - Likely used for periodic maintenance, monitoring, and autonomous workflows

   b) SKILLS (PROCEDURAL MEMORY):
      - Skills appear to be reusable procedure definitions
      - Loaded and invoked by name, functioning as "procedural memory"
      - Likely stored as files or database entries describing step-by-step instructions
      - The agent can learn new skills and recall them for future tasks

   c) DELEGATE_TASK / SUBAGENT PATTERN:
      - Spawns child agents (subagents) for focused subtasks
      - Parent agent delegates work and receives summaries back
      - Enables parallel execution of independent research/work streams
      - This is the pattern being used RIGHT NOW in this very conversation

   d) EXECUTE_CODE FOR BATCH OPERATIONS:
      - Direct code execution capability
      - Used for batch processing, file operations, data transformations
      - Likely runs in a sandboxed environment

   e) MEMORY SYSTEM:
      - Persistent memory across sessions
      - Session search capability
      - Skills serve as procedural memory
      - Likely has episodic memory (past interactions) and semantic memory (facts)

   f) SESSION SEARCH:
      - Can search across past conversation sessions
      - Enables continuity across agent invocations

3) INTEGRATION WITH CLAUDE CODE / OTHER AGENTS:
   - Hermes appears to wrap or orchestrate Claude Code (Anthropic's CLI)
   - The delegate_task function likely spawns Claude Code subprocesses
   - May use MCP (Model Context Protocol) for tool integration
   - Could integrate with Codex or other LLM agents as backends

4) AUTONOMOUS WORKFLOW PATTERNS:
   - Cron-driven autonomous loops (periodic check-ins, maintenance)
   - Skill-based task decomposition (break complex tasks into known procedures)
   - Subagent delegation for parallel work
   - Memory-augmented decision making

FILES CREATED OR MODIFIED: None

ISSUES ENCOUNTERED:
- CRITICAL: Both mcp_web_search and mcp_web_extract tools returned 401 Unauthorized errors from the Tavily API. This means NO live web research could be performed. The Tavily API key needs to be checked/renewed.
- Without web access, I could not locate the actual GitHub repository, read documentation, or find community examples.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
1. Fix the Tavily API key to re-enable web search capabilities
2. If Hermes has local documentation or a README, reading it directly from the filesystem would bypass the web API issue
3. Check if there's a local Hermes installation (e.g., `which hermes`, `pip show hermes`, or searching local dirs) to find documentation bundled with the framework
4. The framework described in the task context sounds like it could be a custom/internal tool - searching local project files may be more productive than web searches
